message:
feat: add V2 sidecar staleness detection with JSONL rehydration fallback and cross-format chain hash verification

When a V2 sidecar falls behind the JSONL source of truth (e.g. after
incremental JSONL-only saves), the session layer now detects this via a
new `v2_sidecar_stale` field and the extracted `is_v2_sidecar_stale()`
helper. On full rehydration before save, stale sidecars trigger a
fallback to JSONL-based rehydration instead of reading from the outdated
V2 store, preventing data loss on non-active branches.

Additionally, `verify_v2_against_jsonl()` now performs a true cross-format
chain hash comparison using SHA-256 (`session_entry_chain_hash_step()`)
rather than relying solely on V2 index integrity validation. This catches
payload-level divergence even when entry IDs and counts match, closing a
gap where tampered or drifted V2 stores could pass migration verification.

Key changes:
- Add `v2_sidecar_stale` field to Session, propagated through Clone and
  all constructors (create, open_jsonl, open_from_v2)
- Extract `is_v2_sidecar_stale()` from inline closure in open path
- Mark sidecar stale after incremental JSONL writes (save_all, append)
- Full rewrite path now checks staleness and falls back to JSONL source
- Add `session_entry_chain_hash_step()` for SHA-256 chain hash computation
- Cross-format verification uses JSONL-derived chain hash vs V2 store hash
- Add `V2_CHAIN_HASH_GENESIS` constant for genesis block hash
- Add test: partial hydration full rewrite uses JSONL when sidecar is stale
- Add test: verify_v2 detects payload mismatch with matching IDs
- Fix node:http shim test: capture header values after setHeader calls
  to match actual evaluation order

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
